ops.title: Retrain journal: dual-write to cc-notes with JSONL authority
ops.body:
## Retrain journal + packaged recipe (as of commit 0358ab3c)

### `promotion.journal()` is the single writer, dual-sink
`cc_steer/retrain/promotion.py::journal()` is the ONE codepath that records a
retrain pass (skip / reject / promote). Keep it that way — every lane
(`retrain_watcher`, `retrain_gate`, `register_watcher`) routes through it.

It writes two sinks:
1. **Authoritative JSONL** at `<state_dir>/retrain/journal.jsonl` (default
   `~/.cc-steer/retrain/journal.jsonl`). This is the source of truth.
2. **Best-effort cc-notes mirror**: `Journal(Path.cwd(), title="cc-steer retrain
   journal", label="retrain").append("<component>: <verdict>")`.

The mirror uses `Path.cwd()` as the repo (no `journal_repo` param is threaded —
the mirror is self-contained inside `journal()`). It degrades silently to a
no-op when cc-notes is missing or the repo is uninitialized, matching
`cc_steer/journal.py::Journal`'s failure posture. This is why the headless
launchd retrain lane (uvx, cwd usually `$HOME`, no cc-notes init) records
nothing to cc-notes but still keeps the JSONL — that is intended, NOT a bug.
When a human runs `cc-steer retrain` from the cc-steer repo, the mirror lands.

**Do not** make the mirror raise or gate the JSONL write on it; the JSONL must
survive any cc-notes failure.

### Tests never touch the real cc-notes ledger
This repo IS cc-notes-initialized, so an unmocked `journal()` call from cwd
would append to the real "cc-steer retrain journal" log. `tests/conftest.py`
has an autouse `hermetic_cc_notes` fixture that intercepts only `cc-notes`
subprocess invocations (degrading them to exit 1) and passes every other
subprocess through. A test that asserts the mirror re-patches `subprocess.run`
with its own spy (see `test_mirrors_line_to_cc_notes_retrain_log`).

### Watcher recipe ships as a packaged asset
There is no `WATCHER_RECIPE` module constant anymore. The E8-winner default
lives in `cc_steer/assets/watcher_recipe.json` and is parsed via
`WatcherRecipe.default()` → `WatcherRecipe.from_json(...)` (full validation
preserved). The CLI `--recipe` stays `default=None`; the watcher dispatch
resolves `WatcherRecipe.default()` when omitted (keeping the gate-lane
`recipe is not None` watcher-only rejection intact — do NOT set the click
default to the asset path, or the gate lane always trips that guard).

**Wheel inclusion matters**: the weekly launchd lane runs
`uvx --from 'cc-steer[retrain]'` with no repo checkout, so the recipe MUST ship
in the wheel. `uv_build` bundles everything under `cc_steer/` (the `assets/`
dir was already shipped for the dashboard); after any change, prove it with
`uv build --wheel && unzip -l dist/*.whl | grep watcher_recipe.json`.
`cc_steer/assets/__init__.py` exposes `WATCHER_RECIPE_PATH` via
`Path(__file__).parent`, the same filesystem idiom the dashboard uses
(`dashboard.py: ASSETS = Path(__file__).with_name("assets")`).
ops.when: Before modifying promotion.journal(), extending retrain logging, changing how the watcher recipe is loaded, or touching the cc-notes integration in the retrain lanes
